description: 19-year-old female browsing for herself — expects fashion/cosmetics lean

messages.content: hey I'm just looking around, want something nice for myself

expected_qualities: uses the user's name Sashika
suggests categories appropriate for a 19-year-old female (fashion, cosmetics, lifestyle)
warm and friendly tone, not corporate
asks one clarifying question to narrow down — does not overwhelm
does not suggest electronics or home goods as first option

description: 45-year-old male looking for electronics — direct product intent

messages.content: I want to buy some electronics, what do you have?

expected_qualities: uses the user's name Ruwan
suggests electronics appropriate for a 45-year-old male
confident recommendations, not a list of generic options
mentions price or value proposition
moves conversation toward a specific product or category

description: Very low budget under 500 LKR — honesty required

messages.content: I only have 400 rupees, what can I get?

expected_qualities: acknowledges the budget honestly — does not pretend expensive items are available
does not make the user feel bad about their budget
suggests realistic options at that price point (snacks, small gifts, cards)
stays warm and helpful in tone
does not upsell aggressively

description: Vague request — just get me something nice

messages.content: just get me something nice

expected_qualities: asks exactly one clarifying question — for who and what occasion
does not show random products without context
keeps the question warm, not interrogative
does not ask multiple questions at once
acknowledges the vagueness naturally

description: Gifting: 25-year-old male sending to 55-year-old mother in Colombo

messages.content: I want to send a gift to my mum in Colombo, she's 55

expected_qualities: recognises this is a gifting scenario
tailors suggestions for a 55-year-old female recipient
asks about occasion or recipient preferences to refine
mentions delivery to Colombo is possible
does not suggest gifts more appropriate for a young person

description: Birthday with tight deadline — tomorrow delivery

messages.content: my mothers birthday is tomorrow and need to buy her a gift, she likes planting

expected_qualities: understands 'planting' means gardening/plants — NOT planners, planning books, or office supplies
shows or asks about plant-related gifts (indoor plants, garden tools, flower seeds, pots)
acknowledges the urgency — tomorrow delivery
does not suggest cakes or generic birthday items as primary recommendation
uses warm tone appropriate for a gifting scenario

description: Parents anniversary — sender unsure what to get

messages.content: it's my parents' anniversary next week and I have no idea what to get them

expected_qualities: recognises both recipients (couple, not individual)
asks at least one question to narrow down — budget, or what they enjoy
suggests couple-appropriate gifts (experience, home items, flowers, dinner)
tone is warm and relatable — speaks like a friend who has been there
does not suggest single-person gifts as primary option

description: Gift from a child to a parent — age gap dynamic

messages.content: I want to buy something for my dad for father's day, he's 48

expected_qualities: treats a 14-year-old respectfully — not condescending
suggests budget-friendly options first — assumes limited funds
tailors suggestion for a 48-year-old male recipient
mentions Father's Day context positively
sweet, encouraging tone appropriate for the relationship

description: Delivery deadline — needs to arrive by Sunday

messages.content: I need something delivered by this Sunday. Is that possible?

expected_qualities: addresses the delivery deadline directly
does not promise delivery without checking or caveating
asks for the delivery city/area to assess feasibility
keeps response concise — this is an availability question, not a product pitch yet
moves toward understanding what they want to buy

description: Same-day delivery to Kandy

messages.content: Can anything get to Kandy today?

expected_qualities: answers the same-day Kandy question directly
does not make up availability — caveats appropriately
asks what they want to send to help narrow down options
if same-day is possible for some items, mentions which categories typically qualify
concise and helpful

description: Caller in London, recipient in Colombo, Vesak next week

messages.content: I'm in London and want to send something to my parents in Colombo for Vesak, it's next week

expected_qualities: acknowledges the international sender context (Kapruka specialises in overseas gifting)
mentions Vesak-appropriate gift ideas (lamp, flowers, sweets, merit card)
does not suggest items that are culturally inappropriate for Vesak
reassures that international delivery is handled well by Kapruka
asks about budget or specific preference to narrow down

description: Multi-item cart: cake + flowers together

messages.content: Can I add a birthday cake and flowers together in one order?

expected_qualities: confirms that multi-item orders are possible on Kapruka
asks for cake preference (flavour, size) or flowers preference (type, colour)
moves conversation toward building the cart
mentions that both will be delivered together
warm and helpful tone

description: Multi-turn cart: add, add another, remove one

messages.content: Add the chocolate cake to my cart
Done! Chocolate Birthday Cake added to your cart. Anything else to add, Tharaka?
also add the flowers
Red Rose Bouquet added too! Your cart now has the cake and flowers. Shall I keep going or do you want to checkout?
actually remove the cake, just flowers is fine

expected_qualities: acknowledges the removal request clearly — confirms cake is removed
updates the cart summary (flowers only now)
does not argue or push back on the removal
offers to proceed to checkout or add something else
tone stays smooth — handles the change naturally

description: Tanglish input: machan I need something for my amma

messages.content: machan I need something for my amma la, her birthday is tomorrow

expected_qualities: responds in Tanglish to match the user's register — NOT formal English
uses casual Sri Lankan tone (machan, la, etc. — at least mimics the register)
acknowledges tomorrow urgency
asks what amma likes or suggests appropriate gifts for a mother
feels like talking to a friend who gets the culture

description: Sinhala script input — full Sinhala response required

messages.content: මගේ දියණියට දවසක් කොළඹ ගිහින් ගෙනියන්න හොඳ තෑග්ගක් ඕනෑ

expected_qualities: responds entirely in Sinhala script — no English sentences
product names and prices may remain in English within the Sinhala response
understands the request: a gift to bring back to their daughter from Colombo
suggests appropriate gifts for a daughter (age unknown — should ask)
warm and natural Sinhala tone — not machine-translated sounding

description: Mid-conversation language switch English to Tanglish

messages.content: I want to buy a gift for my girlfriend
How lovely! What kind of things does she like, Malith — fashion, jewellery, skincare?
she likes chocolates and perfumes la, can you show me something nice machang

expected_qualities: switches to Tanglish register to match the user — 'la', 'machang', casual tone
does NOT continue in formal English after the user switched
shows chocolate + perfume options as requested
uses warm gifting tone (girlfriend gift)
keeps response concise — this is a product show, not an explanation

description: User changes mind after adding to cart

messages.content: Add the red roses to my cart
Red Rose Bouquet added! Great choice, Dilhara. Anything else?
actually, I changed my mind, remove them and show me lilies instead

expected_qualities: removes roses without any friction or re-confirmation request
immediately pivots to showing lily options
one brief acknowledgment ('Got it!') then straight to lilies
does not lecture or ask 'are you sure?'
keeps momentum — handles the change gracefully

description: Product category Kapruka likely doesn't carry — custom furniture

messages.content: I need custom-built wooden furniture for my living room, can you help?

expected_qualities: honest about what Kapruka doesn't carry — does not pretend to have custom furniture
does not apologise excessively — just straightforward
suggests the closest alternative Kapruka does carry (home decor, storage, decorative items)
keeps the user engaged rather than just saying no
one sentence honest + pivot to what IS available

description: User gives conflicting information — says gift for friend, then for self

messages.content: I'm looking for a gift for my friend
Nice! What does your friend enjoy — sports, tech, food? And are they male or female, Gayan?
actually it's for me, I want a new watch

expected_qualities: smoothly pivots from gifting to personal shopping without making it awkward
no confusion — immediately shows watches
does not reference the earlier 'friend' context
shows watches appropriate for a 27-year-old male
concise — just show the watches

description: Specific request with no budget mentioned — perfume

messages.content: I want to buy a perfume for myself

expected_qualities: shows perfumes immediately — clear product intent, no need to ask what they want
range of price points shown — from affordable to premium
tailored for a 25-year-old female (floral, fresh, feminine options)
does not ask budget before showing — show first, they will say if too expensive
confident recommendations with a brief description of each
